title: 10 best herbal creams for shortness of breath

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate symptoms of shortness of breath and make it easier to breathe.

Bronchodilatory: The natural compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum, such as eucalyptol, help open up constricted airways and improve lung function, making it easier to take deep breaths and alleviating shortness of breath.

Antioxidant: The cream's antioxidant properties help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by pollutants, smoke, or other environmental irritants, which can contribute to shortness of breath.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with shortness of breath because of its decongestant properties. The active compounds, particularly eucalyptol, help to break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. The cooling sensation provided by the cream can also help to soothe the respiratory tract, further alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath. By addressing the underlying causes, Eucalyptus globulus creams offer relief for individuals experiencing respiratory distress.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): As a bronchodilator, cineole helps to relax the muscles in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving shortness of breath.
Limonene: Limonene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating shortness of breath.
Catechol: Catechol has a direct effect on the airway muscles, causing them to relax and dilate, which helps to improve airflow and alleviate shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high eucalyptol content, which has expectorant properties that help relieve shortness of breath.
Barks: The bark is often used as it contains compounds like eucalyptol, which can help ease congestion and make breathing easier.
Roots: Some products might include root extracts due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and improve breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c for 4 hours to release the essential oil.
Mix 10% of the distilled essential oil with 90% of a base cream containing 30% beeswax and 70% coconut oil.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Herbal sage creams help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion and wheezing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, allowing for smoother breathing and increased oxygen intake.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with shortness of breath because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs. This allows for easier breathing and reduces congestion. Additionally, sage contains compounds that help to relax the smooth muscles in the airways, making it easier for air to pass through. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy mucus production, Salvia officinalis creams can provide relief for individuals experiencing shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Carnosic acid: As a phenolic diterpenoid, carnosic acid has antioxidant properties that can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, which can contribute to shortness of breath.

Rosmarinic acid: This caffeic acid derivative is a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, thus alleviating shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used in herbal remedies due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could potentially help alleviate respiratory issues.
Flowers: They are used in teas and infusions for their calming effects, which might help with shortness of breath in some cases.
Roots: They are sometimes used in herbal remedies for their anti-inflammatory properties, which could potentially help with respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers by cutting them with scissors in the morning.
Extract the herb by steeping it in 100 ml of cold carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate the liquid from the plant material.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax is melted.
Pour the cooled mixture into small glass jars and seal them tightly after 24 hours of setting time.

remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a natural antiseptic and antibacterial compound found in thyme, helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Cineole: Cineole, a monoterpene present in thyme, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which can help relax airway passages and improve breathing.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, another monoterpene found in thyme, has decongestant properties that can help reduce mucus buildup in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with shortness of breath because of its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, allowing for more efficient airflow and relief from shortness of breath. This makes thyme creams a potential natural remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps with shortness of breath by acting as a bronchodilator, which means it helps to relax and widen the airways, improving breathing.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate helps with shortness of breath by reducing inflammation in the airways and lungs, making it easier to breathe.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid helps with shortness of breath by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used because of their high concentration of thymol, a compound with decongestant properties.
Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are used due to their high thymol content, which helps to relieve respiratory issues such as shortness of breath.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are used because they contain thymol and other compounds that have expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh thyme leaves and stems from the plant to use in the herbal cream recipe.
Dry the thyme leaves and stems in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Combine 20g of dried thyme with 100g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl to create a blend.
Steep the thyme blend in a double boiler at 50c for 4 hours to infuse the coconut oil with herbal properties.
Strain the infused coconut oil through cheesecloth and mix it with 100g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to create the final cream.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Reduces inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate breathing difficulties by relaxing airway constriction and decreasing mucus production.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and lung damage, helping to improve overall lung function and reduce shortness of breath.

Phenolic acids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax airway muscles, reduce bronchospasm, and alleviate symptoms of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and asthma.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with shortness of breath because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways. This can help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath by improving airflow and reducing congestion. Additionally, the cream's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Overall, using herbal Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may provide relief from shortness of breath and promote respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation in the airways and alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath associated with respiratory conditions.
Licoricidin: Licoricidin, a flavonoid glycoside, exhibits anti-asthmatic properties by relaxing bronchial muscles and improving lung function, thereby reducing shortness of breath.

Licopyrocatechol: Licopyrocatechol, a phenolic compound, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect the airways and alleviate oxidative stress, contributing to relief from shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate respiratory issues.
Barks: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and shortness of breath.
Leaves: They are used due to their flavonoid content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe respiratory issues and reduce shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Steep 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 500ml of hot water for 30 minutes to create a decoction.
Combine 200ml of the decoction with 200ml of distilled water and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Add 5g of vegetable oil and 5g of emulsifying wax to the mixture and stir well.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a storage container.

remedy_properties: Relaxation Inducing Properties: Peppermint creams can help relax the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing shortness of breath due to their calming effect on the respiratory system.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The menthol in peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways, which can constrict breathing passages and cause shortness of breath.

Expectorant Properties: Peppermint's natural expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating shortness of breath caused by congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with shortness of breath because of its natural expectorant properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams helps thin mucus in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals with respiratory issues such as bronchitis or asthma. Additionally, peppermint's decongestant properties help open airways, allowing for more efficient oxygen exchange, which can help alleviate shortness of breath and improve overall respiratory function.

Menthone: Menthone has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Limonene: Limonene has decongestant properties, helping to clear mucus from the airways and reduce congestion, which can contribute to shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have a decongestant effect and help relieve respiratory issues.
Stems: They contain a similar composition to leaves, providing relief from shortness of breath by easing respiratory congestion.
Buds: They have a higher concentration of menthol and menthone than leaves, making them effective in treating respiratory issues such as shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves when the plant is in full bloom.
Combine the harvested leaves with 1/2 cup of distilled water in a saucepan and heat.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain and discard the solids immediately.
Mix the resulting liquid with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and is ready to use.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate breathing difficulties by allowing airways to open up more easily.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in rosemary cream loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing shortness of breath.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 10g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in 100ml of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Combine the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 5g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at 40 degrees celsius for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal Kansas coneflower creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier for oxygen to reach the body's tissues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 cups of echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall season with scissors or a root fork.
Dry the harvested roots in a dehydrator at 95°f for 8-12 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 1 cup of dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of the ground root powder in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and reduced congestion, which can alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of foeniculum vulgare seeds on a dry morning to prevent moisture damage.
Dry the foeniculum vulgare seeds in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 24 hours.
Grind 20g of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20g of foeniculum vulgare powder with 100g of emulsifying wax and 50g of coconut oil.
Whip the mixture in a double boiler at 60c for 30 minutes until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 percent of the calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when they are dry and free from dew.
Dry the calendula officinalis flowers in a single layer at 30 to 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Mix 20 percent of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 80 percent of a base oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Use a hand blender to mix the oil and calendula officinalis mixture for 10 to 15 minutes until it is well combined.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in a glass container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath by allowing for easier breathing.

Calming and sedative properties: The calming effects of lavender, particularly its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, can help alleviate feelings of panic and discomfort associated with shortness of breath, making it easier to breathe naturally.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds may help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals and infection, which can contribute to chronic respiratory conditions like bronchitis or pneumonia that can cause shortness of breath.

Stems: They contain volatile oils and flavonoids that may help relax the airways and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and clean them thoroughly with a fine-mesh strainer.
Combine 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day to facilitate infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Whip 1 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tickseed creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain a unique combination of herbs that work synergistically to relax the airways and reduce inflammation. The herbs, such as mullein and lobelia, have a soothing effect on the lungs and bronchial tubes, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, tickseed creams can help to thin mucus, allowing for better airflow and relieving congestion. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphorbia hirta leaves and stems, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pests.
Dry the harvested plant material using a clean towel or air drying rack for at least 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried euphorbia hirta with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or thermometer-controlled water bath at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding solids and storing the resulting cream in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain the essential oil of lemon verbena, which has a natural relaxing effect on the bronchial passages. The oil's calming properties help to soothe and open up constricted airways, making it easier for oxygen to flow in and out of the lungs. Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in lemon verbena help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion associated with shortness of breath.

Barks: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax airway muscles and improve lung function.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried verbena officinalis leaves measuring 10 grams and infuse them in boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard solids.
Mix 50 milliliters of the infused liquid with 150 milliliters of coconut oil and let it cool down to room temperature.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil until well combined and smooth in texture.
Blend the cooled mixture from step 3 with the whipped beeswax mixture until a thick creamy consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with shortness of breath because its natural compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, have been shown to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the airways. Anxiolytic: Lemon balm's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property can help alleviate stress and anxiety-related breathing difficulties, such as hyperventilation or rapid shallow breathing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source, measuring 20 grams for the recipe.
Combine the dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of organic sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler set at medium heat for 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids after 15 minutes.
Mix 50 milliliters of the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain bioactive compounds that have a profound effect on respiratory health. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger cream help to relax the airways, reducing inflammation and constriction caused by conditions like bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, the warming sensation provided by the cream can help to loosen mucus, making it easier to breathe. Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of ginger help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion that may be contributing to shortness of breath.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh ginger roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the ginger roots in a warm oven at 60°c for 12 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Grind 100g of dried ginger into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20% of the powdered ginger with 80% coconut oil and 5% beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes to ensure the ingredients are fully incorporated and cooled to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams help with shortness of breath because they contain curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways. This can help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), which often cause shortness of breath. By reducing inflammation and opening up air passages, turmeric creams can improve lung function and make it easier to breathe, providing relief from shortness of breath and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's potent anti-inflammatory compound curcumin helps reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Bronchodilatory: The active compounds in turmeric, such as curcumin and turmerone, have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle and increase airflow, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate bronchial inflammation and reduces airway constriction.
Roots: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, roots have been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
remedy_recipe: Grate one teaspoon of dried turmeric root into a small bowl containing two tablespoons of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over medium heat for 10 minutes until it reaches 100°f.
Add five drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine with the turmeric-infused oil.
Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature before whipping with an electric mixer for two minutes.
Transfer the whipped cream to a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to seven days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain a unique combination of natural ingredients that work synergistically to relax the airways and improve lung function. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing, while its expectorant properties help loosen mucus and promote coughing up impurities. Additionally, the cream's antioxidants help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, further supporting respiratory health and reducing symptoms of shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Passionflower: Passionflower helps to calm the nervous system, which can contribute to shortness of breath by reducing anxiety and stress-induced respiratory issues.
Marshmallow root: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the lungs, helping to reduce inflammation and ease breathing difficulties.
Lavender: Lavender oil has a calming effect on the respiratory system, which can help to relax bronchial muscles and improve airflow, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate bronchial constriction and relieve shortness of breath.
Fruits: Contain a high concentration of vitamin C, which has antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and ease respiratory distress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passionflower flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Mix one cup of dried flowers with two cups of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily, allowing the oil to infuse.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding solids after 24 hours.
Whip the infused oil with one tablespoon of beeswax and two teaspoons of coconut oil until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Betony creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain the natural herb Stachys officinalis, which has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. The cream's anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties help to reduce congestion and ease breathing, making it an effective remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). By soothing the airways and reducing inflammation, betony creams can help to relieve the tightness and discomfort associated with shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Betony's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to shortness of breath by allowing for easier airflow and reducing bronchial constriction.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of betony help to relax bronchial muscles, which can become constricted during an asthma attack or other respiratory conditions, leading to shortness of breath.

Expectorant property: Betony's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier for the body to expel and reducing the feeling of shortness of breath caused by excess mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Betony roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate respiratory issues associated with shortness of breath.
Barks: The barks of the betony plant contain compounds that have been traditionally used to soothe bronchial inflammation, making it an effective ingredient for calming breathing difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried stachys officinalis leaves from reputable suppliers in amounts of 20 grams for every 100ml cream base.
Combine the dried stachys officinalis leaves with 70% isopropyl alcohol in a ratio of 1:5 and steep for 24 hours to create a tincture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes of sedimentation has occurred.
Mix 20% w/w beeswax and 80% w/w coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth, then cool to 65°c for adding the tincture.
Combine the cooled wax-oil mixture with the stachys officinalis tincture at a ratio of 1:3 and stir thoroughly before pouring into sterile glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with shortness of breath because they contain the herb valerian root, which has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. The cream is absorbed through the skin, where it works to soothe respiratory passages and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe. By calming the body's response to stress and anxiety, valerian creams help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath, providing relief from conditions such as asthma, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and sleep apnea.
remedy_properties: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a primary active compound in valerian root, helps to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to improved airflow and reduced shortness of breath.

Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, another key component of valerian, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that may help to calm bronchial spasms and open up airways, alleviating symptoms of shortness of breath.

Valerin: Valerin, a volatile oil found in valerian root, has been shown to exhibit expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, which can help to alleviate congestion and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of volatile compounds, including sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, which help to relax the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways and relieve bronchial constriction, making them useful for treating shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valeriana officinalis root extract measuring 2 tablespoons and crush it into fine powder.
Combine 4 ounces of beeswax, 6 ounces of coconut oil, and 2 ounces of olive oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Add the crushed valeriana officinalis powder to the mixture and stir well for approximately 10 minutes until smooth consistency achieved.
Transfer the mixture to a double boiler or a heat-proof glass measuring cup placed over simmering water and stir occasionally for 20-30 minutes until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for about an hour before transferring it to an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for shortness of breath are topical ointments containing plant extracts that help alleviate respiratory issues by soothing the airways and improving oxygen flow. The best herbs for shortness of breath include Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea purpurea, Astragalus membranaceus, Panax ginseng, Zingiber officinale, Ephedra sinica, Eucalyptus globulus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Withania somnifera, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, shortness of breath can hinder daily activities, lead to fatigue and stress, and even exacerbate underlying medical conditions, negatively impacting overall quality of life and mental well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shortness of breath that are treated with herbal creams are respiratory conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint, eucalyptus, and thyme may help relieve congestion, ease coughing, and open airways. Other conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and rosacea that cause skin irritation and itching may also be treated with herbal creams, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healthy skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with shortness of breath is a blend of Peppermint, Eucalyptus, and Ginger. Peppermint helps to open airways and reduce inflammation, while Eucalyptus has decongestant properties that ease respiratory congestion. Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds aid in reducing bronchial inflammation and promoting oxygen flow. Together, these creams create a synergistic effect, providing quick relief from shortness of breath and promoting overall respiratory health. Regular application can help alleviate symptoms and improve breathing.
